entries.intro: Something's been eating my cabbages again. I found half a head chewed up this morning.
details: There are little tracks around the bed, but I can't tell what's making them. Too big for a mouse, too small for a deer.
I put a fence up last season. Whatever it is just goes around it.
reactions: I'm not even angry anymore. I'm just impressed by how persistent it is.

statCheck.pass: Those tracks sound like a groundhog. They'll dig right under a fence unless you bury the posts deep.
statCheck.fail: I'm no tracker. Could be anything out there.

entries.intro: The birds have been loud this week. I mean really loud: starts before dawn.
details: There's one in particular that sounds like it's screaming a question over and over. No answer ever comes.
I used to like birdsong. Now I just want five more minutes of sleep.
reactions: My neighbor says it means the weather's changing. I say it means the birds are annoying.

entries.intro: I found a patch of wild berries along the trail the other day. Ate so many I made myself sick.
details: They were perfect: dark, sweet, stained my fingers purple. The kind you only find once a season.
I meant to pick some to bring home but I just kept eating them on the spot.
reactions: Totally worth it. I'll go back tomorrow if the birds haven't stripped the bushes.

entries.intro: The creek has been running low lately. Usually it's up to my ankles this time of year.
details: The rocks at the bottom are showing that haven't been dry in years. Covered in pale mud.
My garden's feeling it too. I've been hauling water from the well instead.
reactions: It always comes back eventually, but the waiting makes you nervous.

statCheck.pass: The water table drops when we get a dry spring. The creek's fed by runoff: it'll come back once we get a few good rains.
statCheck.fail: I don't understand where water comes from or where it goes. It just does.

entries.intro: There's a fox that comes through every evening along the fence line. Same time, same path.
details: It walks right along the fence like it owns the place. Doesn't even look at me anymore.
I've started leaving a bit of meat out. My wife says I'm encouraging it: she's probably right.
reactions: I like having it around. Makes the evening a little less dull.

statCheck.pass: Foxes are creatures of habit. They'll use the same trails for generations if nothing pushes them off.
statCheck.fail: I don't know much about foxes. I just like watching this one.

entries.intro: The flies have been unbearable lately. I can't eat outside without swallowing one.
details: I've tried herbs, smoke, waving my arms like a fool: nothing works.
They're worse this year than I ever remember. Must be something rotting nearby that I can't find.
reactions: I've given up eating outdoors. It's not worth the battle.

entries.intro: I pulled up a carrot this morning that was shaped exactly like a little person. Arms, legs, the whole thing.
details: I showed it to my neighbor and she said she'd seen weirder. Hard to believe, but I didn't argue.
It happens when the root hits a stone and has to grow around it. Still funny every time.
reactions: I almost didn't want to eat it. Almost.

statCheck.pass: Carrots fork when the taproot meets resistance: a stone, hard clay, even another root. The soil around here is rocky, so it happens a lot.
statCheck.fail: No idea why they grow like that. Just one of those things.

entries.intro: The leaves are already turning. Feels early this year.
details: There's a big oak I pass every day and it's gone half gold. Usually doesn't do that for another few weeks.
The nights have been cold, though. Could be that's pushing things along.
reactions: I'm not ready for it. Summer went too fast.
